title: Lines Upon The Rev. Mr. C ---- 's Impromptu Compositions Of Some Of Bowles's Sonnets.
content:
No sweeter verse did e'er inspire
    A kindred Muse with all its fire;
    Nor sweeter strains could Music lend,
    To sooth the sorrows of her friend.

    Associate Genius bids them flow
    With sounds that give a charm to woe;
    We weep as tho' it were our own,
    As if our hearts were play'd upon.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Carr (Sir) delivers a powerful performance in "Lines Upon The Rev. Mr. C ---- 's Impromptu Compositions Of Some Of Bowles's Sonnets."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
